narrative:
The week opened with a stress score of 24 on Monday before easing to 22 on Tuesday and dropping sharply to 18 on Wednesday, its lowest point of the period. Thursday saw a brief rebound to 26, the week's peak, before settling back down to 16 on Friday. The regime held firmly in business-as-usual territory throughout, with no material escalation into stress or fragmentation, suggesting the mid-week softness was transient rather than structural.

High yield spreads drifted tighter across the week, moving from 283 basis points on Monday to 279 by Friday, albeit with some intraday oscillation that saw spreads touch 272 on Thursday before widening again. The modest spread compression broadly confirmed the low-stress equity backdrop, with credit offering no contradictory signal. Investment grade spreads were not flagged as diverging, reinforcing a picture of contained risk appetite rather than one being aggressively extended.

Two deep value signals appeared across the week. BlackRock was flagged on Monday in a wait posture, suggesting the screen identified potential value but conditions were not yet sufficiently favourable to act. Walmart appeared on both Thursday and Friday, though the confidence designation remains unresolved, warranting caution before drawing a firm conclusion on entry timing.
